title: [lichen_l2.c:565-566] net_pkt_cursor_init + net_pkt_read pattern assumes single fragment
description:
Gap found: net_pkt fragment handling not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Zephyr net_pkt API usage assumed correct.

The actual bug: Lines 565-566 use net_pkt_cursor_init() followed by net_pkt_read() to linearize the packet into tx_ipv6_buf. This pattern works correctly for fragmented packets - net_pkt_read() handles multi-fragment packets internally.

However, the check at line 553 'pkt_len > sizeof(tx_ipv6_buf)' uses net_pkt_get_len() which returns total length across all fragments. This is correct.

The subtle concern: if net_pkt_read() fails mid-way through a fragmented packet (e.g., corrupted fragment pointer), tx_ipv6_buf contains partial data. The error is propagated (lines 567-570) but tx_ipv6_buf is not zeroed on failure. If HAVE_LICHEN_LINK is false (raw mode), this could leak partial packet data on retry.

Actually, on failure we return error and don't use the buffer. No leak. Priority 4 - verified correct.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation comment explaining that net_pkt_read() handles multi-fragment packets transparently via cursor iteration across all net_buf fragments
